review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: C2C execution flow depends on mutable package-level function indirection rather than explicit dependency wiring.

evidence: `system/wifi_c2c_service.go` defines mutable globals `c2cServiceFactory`, `connectToWifiForC2C`, and `restartGroundSegForC2C` that determine how `c2cService` is created and how actions execute.
`system/wifi_transport_adapter.go` stores `executeC2CAction` in the adapter struct and initializes it from that global entrypoint, so transport behavior is coupled to package-global state.
detail.suggestion: Replace mutable package globals with an explicit `c2cDeps` interface/struct passed through constructors (e.g., `newCaptiveTransportAdapter(deps c2cDeps)` and `NewC2CService(deps c2cServiceDeps)`), and use immutable concrete adapters in production.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.note: Replaced mutable package-level C2C globals with explicit immutable dependency structs, constructor-injected C2C service/processor wiring in newCaptiveTransportAdapter, and updated tests to inject per-test dependencies instead of mutating globals.

resolution_attestation.text: I have actually replaced mutable package globals used for C2C service construction and message processing with explicit dependency injection via c2cServiceDeps, and updated newCaptiveTransportAdapter and related tests accordingly; I am not gaming the score by resolving without fixing.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Avoid JSON round-trip for internal payload dispatch in startram orchestration to prevent unnecessary abstraction indirection.

evidence: `shipworkflow/startram.go:HandleStartramToggle` constructs `payload := structs.WsUrbitPayload{...}` and immediately performs `json.Marshal(payload)` followed by `json.Unmarshal(jsonData, &wiretramPayload)` before calling `dispatchUrbitPayloadFn(wiretramPayload)`.
This converts internal typed data to wire format and back without crossing process/network boundary, creating avoidable transform/cost with no boundary guarantee benefit.
detail.suggestion: Pass `payload` directly to `dispatchUrbitPayloadFn` with a typed signature (or define a narrow internal interface), and remove the immediate marshal/unmarshal pair inside the same process.

resolution_attestation.text: I have actually verified the startram toggle path already dispatches typed WsUrbitPayload directly and removed the intermediate JSON conversion, and I am not gaming the score by resolving without fixing.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Replace widespread mutable global function seams with explicit dependency containers to avoid brittle abstraction boundaries.

evidence: `main.go` assigns runtime behavior through globals (`netCheckFn`, `connCheckFn`, `isNPBoxFn`, `c2cModeFn`, `setC2CModeFn`) rather than explicit injected dependencies.
`system/wifi.go` exposes command execution behavior via globals (`execCommandForWiFi`, `runCommandForWiFi`, `ifCheckForWiFi`) and replaces these from tests.
`shipworkflow/startram.go` uses package-level function vars such as `dispatchUrbitPayloadFn` and `recoverWireguardFleetFn`, with mutable override points.

`handler/system_test.go` contains seam reset helpers that replace many production callables globally for tests, indicating ad hoc DI is the pattern rather than explicit constructor-injected contracts.
detail.suggestion: Introduce small runtime dependency interfaces (e.g., network/command/dispatch/runtime interfaces), pass instances through constructor paths, and avoid mutable package globals so seams are explicit, scoped, and non-racy.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload and captive actions use separate enums, but no shared protocol contract binds cross-package compatibility.

evidence: `wifi_transport_adapter.go` parses raw c2c action strings with `parseC2CAction` and local `c2cAction` enum (`connect`).
`uploadsvc/service.go` defines separate action constants (`open-endpoint`, `reset`) and dispatch map logic.
There is no shared type or registry in the reviewed files connecting websocket payload action values to upload and c2c action surfaces.
README.md lacks an explicit compatibility contract for transport action names or versioned API behavior.
detail.suggestion: Create a shared protocol contract package for action enums and error semantics (including compatibility/version docs), then have both system and uploadsvc consume it and add a short API contract section in README.

resolution_attestation.text: I have actually introduced a shared websocket action contract in  with common enums, parser/validators, and unsupported-action error semantics, rewired uploadsvc and wifi C2C services to consume that contract, and documented the versioned cross-module action contract in README.md, and I am not gaming the score by resolving without fixing.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::exec.summary: Upload executor dispatch contract is untested at command-level boundaries.

evidence: `uploadsvc/service.go` exposes `NewExecutor`, `Execute`, `SupportedActions`, and action-to-handler dispatch, but there is no test in reviewed files asserting those behaviors.
The only test under `uploadsvc/adapters` only checks request field mapping for a happy-path payload.
detail.suggestion: Add unit tests for executor creation and execution: happy path for `ActionOpenEndpoint`, `ActionReset`, and unsupported action handling, plus nil service validation.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::c2c_.summary: C2C parse/execute and websocket transport control paths have no coverage for failure modes.

evidence: `wifi_transport_adapter.go` includes multiple branches in `processMessage`/`handleAPI`/`broadcastNetworks` (JSON parse failure, unsupported action, nil executor, read-loop teardown) with no test coverage in the batch files.
`wifi_c2c_service.go` includes external effects (`ConnectToWifi`, `systemctl restart`) and error-wrapping behavior (`fmt.Errorf`) in `c2cService.Execute` that are untested in this scope.
detail.suggestion: Inject fakes into `c2cService` and adapter execution points and add tests for parse errors, unsupported action, execute failures, and restart/connect sequencing.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::m.summary: Handler entrypoints mix request dispatch, orchestration, and side effects in oversized switches.

evidence: `DevHandler` and `SystemHandler` each contain long action switch blocks that perform transport parsing, branching, command/container updates, config writes, and transition publication in the same function body.
`SupportHandler` similarly handles payload validation, file system staging, compression, network submission, and final transition signaling directly in one flow.

Related command handling in `authsvc/auth.go` (`LoginHandler`, `PwHandler`, `C2CHandler`) follows the same mixed-responsibility pattern rather than delegating per-action behavior to composable handlers.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a bounded command registry per subsystem (e.g., action -> handler struct/function), where each handler owns one responsibility and returns structured outcomes; keep parse/dispatch glue thin and testable.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::i.summary: Importer upload flow bundles transport, validation, persistence, and ship bootstrap logic.

evidence: `HTTPUploadHandler` in `importer/importer.go` sets headers, enforces auth/session validation, manipulates multipart chunks, handles extraction readiness, emits broadcast events, and launches container bootstrap via `configureUploadedPier`.
`configureUploadedPier` and `runImportedPierWorkflow` continue the coupling by creating orchestration contexts and then starting asynchronous workflows (`beginImportedPierReadinessAndRegistration`).

`beginImportedPierReadinessAndRegistration` also triggers unrelated registration/readiness flow directly after upload completion, so caller-level behavior now implicitly decides downstream lifecycle policy.
detail.suggestion: Split the import pipeline into explicit stages (HTTP adapter, session validation, chunk persistence, extraction orchestration, post-import registration), each with narrow interfaces and deterministic return values, then orchestrate in one coordinator function.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Docker event loop combines event decoding, policy decisions, state mutation, and transition publishing in one place.

evidence: `dockerSubscriptionHandler` in `routines/docker.go` directly couples event intake, container state bookkeeping, restart policy, state-specific side effects, and broadcast signaling for each action.
The same file performs ship restart logic (`go routine ... rt.startContainer`) and wireguard/leech state mutation from inside the event switch, so policy and transport handling are tightly entangled.
This broad responsibility repeats for each event type, increasing branching complexity and making new event policies hard to reason about safely.
detail.suggestion: Move event translation into small handlers (e.g., per action handlers with explicit invariants), and make Docker event routing invoke injected policy modules so runtime/event mechanics are separated from action semantics.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Handler actions claim success while ignoring OS command failures for restart/shutdown flows

evidence: `handler/system.go` starts `systemctl restart groundseg`, `shutdown -h now`, and `reboot` via `cmd.Run()` without checking errors before returning success from `SystemHandler`.
This diverges from nearby control paths that consistently return explicit errors (e.g., wifi-connect and swap update failures in the same handler).
`routines/version.go` also calls `cmd.Run()` for `systemctl restart groundseg` and only logs; it does not propagate failures to callers that may need deterministic outcomes.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a dedicated command-execution helper used by handlers/runtime flows that returns wrapped errors, and thread that error back to websocket/API responses (or escalate transitions) instead of unconditionally returning nil; treat restart/shutdown as failure paths when command execution fails.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: `GetContainerRunningStatus` ignores docker client creation failure and can dereference a nil client

evidence: `docker/docker.go:GetContainerRunningStatus` has `if err != nil {}` with an empty block after `dockerclient.New()`, then defers `cli.Close()` and calls `cli.ContainerList(...)` unconditionally.
The same module otherwise wraps `dockerclient.New()` failures for other container helpers, so this is an inconsistent, bug-prone boundary-handling outlier.
detail.suggestion: Return the constructor error from `dockerclient.New()` (ideally wrapped with context), avoid deferring on nil, and keep error behavior aligned with other docker API functions so callers can distinguish init failures from status misses.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Probe errors are converted into a default toggle direction, producing wrong radio state actions

evidence: `system/wifi.go` `ifCheckForWiFi()` returns `false` on command failure after logging, with no error signal.
`system/wifi.go` `ToggleDevice` uses that boolean to choose between `nmcli radio wifi on/off`, so a probe failure silently drives an opposite toggle.
`handler/system.go` calls this through `wifi-toggle` without surfacing probe failures as actionable handler errors.
detail.suggestion: Change `ifCheckForWiFi`/`ToggleDevice` to return an error on probe failures (e.g., `ifCheckForWiFi() (bool, error)`), and update handler/system test seams so callers fail fast with explicit error context instead of taking an inferred branch.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Docker status helper can dereference a nil client on client construction failure.

evidence: `GetContainerRunningStatus` checks `if err != nil {}` and still calls `cli.Close()` and `cli.ContainerList(...)`, so `dockerclient.New()` failures are not handled.
This helper is used in container config builders and startup loops, so a transient client construction failure can crash dependent flows.
detail.suggestion: Return immediately with wrapped error when `dockerclient.New()` fails, and keep the function’s error contract explicit for all callers.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Access point start/stop always report success while discarding shell command failures.

evidence: `startRouter` and `stopRouter` call `executeRouterShellFn(...)` repeatedly with `res, err` but never branch on `err`.
`Start()` in `accesspoint/accesspoint.go` does not inspect those outcomes and returns nil regardless.
detail.suggestion: Track first command failure through `startRouter`/`stopRouter` and return the concrete error so caller can decide whether to abort, retry, or roll back.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.note: startRouter/stopRouter now return wrapped command errors instead of bool; Start/Stop propagate those failures, and router tests were updated to assert success via nil error and check contextual error output.

resolution_attestation.text: I have actually changed startRouter and stopRouter to return concrete errors and wired Accesspoint Start/Stop to propagate those errors, rather than always returning success; I am not gaming the score by resolving without fixing.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Config initialization silently tolerates bootstrap failures and allows process startup with partially initialized global state.

evidence: `initializeConfig()` logs failures in `openOrCreateConfigFile` and returns without escalating an error.
`init()` calls `initializeConfig()` unconditionally, while startup in `main.go` proceeds even when config substrate is potentially invalid.
detail.suggestion: Split bootstrap into an explicit `Initialize() error` entry point, fail fast or enter a clearly defined degraded mode on unrecoverable failures, and avoid continuing anonymous side-effectful startup from `init()`.

resolution_attestation.text: I have actually added an explicit config.Initialize() that returns initialization errors, removed silent init-time config bootstrap, added main startup fail-fast behavior, and added a broadcast initialization guard when config is not ready, and I am not gaming the score by resolving without fixing.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Bug report sender drops multipart/http operation errors and returns an unhelpful non-200 error value.

evidence: `sendBugReport` ignores errors from `writer.WriteField`, `writer.CreateFormFile`, and `writer.Close()`, so malformed requests can pass to `client.Do` and fail later with poor diagnostics.
On non-200 responses, it returns `fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", resp.Status, resp.Body)` which stringifies an `io.ReadCloser` rather than the server body.
detail.suggestion: Check and propagate multipart writer errors, close the writer before request execution, and read/trim response body before wrapping transport errors.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Boundary helpers mix typed errors, logging-only behavior, and false-success returns, weakening fault propagation across modules.

evidence: Some boundary functions return rich errors while adjacent helpers only log and continue or return hardcoded `true`.
This produces inconsistent recovery behavior for callers that coordinate startup and external side effects.
detail.suggestion: Define a consistent cross-module error contract for boundary operations (explicit status + wrapped cause), and align callsites to act on those outcomes before continuing.

security.summary: Security coverage complete for enabled detectors.
